hints: The 12-sided die averages 6.5, so keep the d8 only if it beats 6.5 (a 7 or 8).
Apply ItГґ to e^{Оєt}X_t; the drift becomes ОєОёe^{Оєt}dt and the noise is a mean-zero ItГґ integral.
solution: By ItГґ, $d(e^{\kappa t}X_t)=e^{\kappa t}(\kappa\theta\,dt-\sigma\sqrt{X_t}\,dW_t)$. Taking expectations (the ItГґ integral has mean $0$): $e^{\kappa T}\mathbb{E}[X_T]-x=\theta(e^{\kappa T}-1)$, so $\mathbb{E}[X_T]=\theta+(x-\theta)e^{-\kappa T}$. With the given values, $2+3e^{-2}$, giving $A=2,B=3,C=-2$ and $ABC=-12.$

hints: For Aв€’B: your bid = bid_A в€’ ask_B, your ask = ask_A в€’ bid_B.
The final length is a run of evens ending in a 1, conditioned on no 3 or 5 occurring in that run.
solution: The final sequence is the successful run: evens then a terminating $1$, with no $3$/$5$. $P(\text{length}=k,\text{success})=(\tfrac12)^{k-1}\tfrac16$ and $P(\text{success})=\tfrac13$. So $\mathbb{E}[\text{length}\mid\text{success}]=\dfrac{\tfrac16\sum_k k(\tfrac12)^{k-1}}{1/3}=\dfrac{(1/6)\cdot4}{1/3}=2.$

hints: Digits(m)=вЊЉlogв‚Ѓв‚Ђ mвЊ‹+1. Find digit counts of 2^1000 and 5^1000.
solution: The digit count is $\lfloor\log_{10}m\rfloor+1$: $2^{1000}$ has $\lfloor1000\log_{10}2\rfloor+1=302$ digits and $5^{1000}$ has $699$. Concatenated, $n$ has $302+699=1001$ digits (matching $2^{1000}5^{1000}=10^{1000}$).

hints: This is a PГіlya urn: starting 1 made, 1 missed, the success rule makes the made-count uniform.
The final number of goals is uniform over its 99 possible values (1 to 99).
solution: Starting with one make and one miss, the rule 'score with probability = current fraction made' is a PГіlya urn, under which the final number of goals is uniformly distributed over its possible values $1,\dots,99$. Hence $P(\text{exactly }66)=\tfrac1{99}.$

hints: Track expected final tails h_t; h_t=Вјh_{tв€’2}+ВЅh_{tв€’1}+Вјh_t with h_0=0,h_1=1.
h_2=2/3, h_3=7/9, h_4=20/27; average over t~Binom(4,ВЅ): E[final tails]=20/27.
solution: Let $h_t$ be expected final tails from $t$ tails: $h_t=\tfrac14h_{t-2}+\tfrac12h_{t-1}+\tfrac14h_t$, giving $h_2=\tfrac23,h_3=\tfrac79,h_4=\tfrac{20}{27}$. Averaging over $t\sim\mathrm{Binom}(4,\tfrac12)$ gives $\mathbb{E}[\text{final tails}]=\tfrac{20}{27}$, so $\mathbb{E}[\text{heads}]=4-\tfrac{20}{27}=\tfrac{88}{27}.$

hints: After removing a pair: one rank has 2 cards, nine ranks have 4 each (38 total).
solution: After removing a matched pair, the deck has $38$ cards: one rank with $2$, nine ranks with $4$. The number of matching-pair draws is $\binom22+9\binom42=1+54=55$ out of $\binom{38}{2}=703$, so $P=\tfrac{55}{703}.$

hints: Multiple of 15 в‡’ divisible by 5 (ends in 0) and by 3 (digit sum divisible by 3).
Digits are 0/1, so the digit sum (number of 1s) must be a multiple of 3 вЂ” at least three 1s.
solution: It must end in $0$ (divisible by $5$) and have digit sum divisible by $3$; with only $1$s and $0$s that means at least three $1$s. The smallest such number is $1110.$

hints: Count solutions of Xв‚Ѓ+Xв‚‚+Xв‚ѓ=N with each in {0,вЂ¦,N}: C(N+2,2) (caps don't bind).
solution: The number of ways to write $X_1+X_2+X_3=N$ with each in $\{0,\dots,N\}$ is $\binom{N+2}{2}$, so $p_N=\dfrac{\binom{N+2}{2}}{(N+1)^3}=\dfrac{N+2}{2(N+1)^2}$. Then $Np_N\to\dfrac12.$
The number of distinct arrangements is the multinomial $\dfrac{19!}{3!\,7!\,9!}=11085360.$

hints: ОЈ over compositions of n into k parts of the multinomial = k^n (assign each of n items to one of k bins).
solution: Summing all multinomials $\binom{7}{b_1,b_2,b_3,b_4}$ counts all ways to place $7$ distinct items into $4$ boxes, which is $4^7=16384.$
